title: Metadata Recovery and Timeline Builder
description: Click simulated evidence photos to recover their EXIF metadata (camera make/model, capture date and time, GPS coordinates in decimal degrees and DMS, and device time zone), then add photos to a chronological timeline and plot them on a mini map. Time-zone inconsistencies are flagged in red so the student learns to question a timestamp before trusting it.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Digital Forensics
EXIF Metadata
Cybercrime Investigation
Evidence Timeline
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — apply EXIF metadata recovery to extract timestamps and GPS coordinates and construct an event timeline.

educational.topic: Recovering EXIF metadata and building a chronological evidence timeline
learningObjectives: Apply EXIF metadata recovery to extract the capture timestamp, GPS coordinates, and device time zone from a digital photo.
Convert GPS coordinates between decimal degrees and degrees/minutes/seconds (DMS) and plot them on a map.
Construct a chronological timeline from multiple evidence files and detect time-zone inconsistencies that cast doubt on a timestamp.
educational.prerequisites: Awareness that digital photos carry embedded EXIF metadata; basic latitude/longitude and 24-hour clock concepts

pedagogical.interactionPattern: The student clicks any of eight simulated evidence photos in a file-browser panel; the selected photo's EXIF metadata loads into a readout showing camera make/model, capture date and time, GPS coordinates in both decimal and DMS, and the device time zone. 'Add to Timeline' places the photo on a chronological timeline that auto-sorts by capture time; 'Plot on Map' toggles a mini map that pins the selected photo and all timeline photos by GPS; 'Export Timeline' prints a formatted chronological report to the console; 'Clear' empties the timeline.
pedagogical.assessment: Investigative workflow practice: the student must read metadata, recognize when a device time zone disagrees with the case home zone (flagged in red on the EXIF panel, the timeline, and the map), and assemble a defensible chronological sequence rather than simply viewing facts.
pedagogical.modelType: Data-driven model: each photo is a record with timestamp, time zone, GPS, and camera fields; the timeline is computed by sorting added records by capture time, the map projects GPS onto a normalized grid, and an anomaly flag fires whenever a photo's time zone differs from the case home zone.
controls: Clickable evidence photo icons (8 simulated photos)
